global_context.scene_description: A cinematic real-but-comedic scene of a Phoenix domestic crisis: a hot living room where the AC has just died on a 110°+ Phoenix day. The husband (white male, mid-30s, balding slightly or short hair, dad-bod, white tank top — the kind that sticks to skin in heat — sweat visibly soaking through, gym shorts) is fully sprawled on his back across the couch, defeated, towel draped over his face, one arm hanging off the side. He looks like he's surrendered to the heat. The wife (white woman, mid-30s, brown ponytail, tank top, denim shorts, sweat at her hairline, frustration written all over her face) stands in front of the couch with one hand on her hip and the other pointing accusingly at an outdoor AC condenser unit visible through a window — mouth open in a yell, mid-sentence, eyes wide with frustration. She is not amused. The Phoenix exterior visible through the window is BRUTALLY hot — bleached pale sky, intense sun, heat shimmer rising from the patio. A weak desk fan oscillates uselessly in the corner. A puddle of sweat on the wood floor. An empty Costco-sized bag of ice on the counter, melting. The air feels thick. Lighting: harsh, hot Phoenix afternoon sun pouring through the windows, slight orange-amber tint suggesting heat — NOT soft natural light. Mood: real-but-comedic Phoenix domestic comedy, the kind of scene every Phoenix homeowner has lived. NO text overlay needed — the situation IS the visual story. Cinematic, like a still from a comedy film, NOT a stock photo.

composition.framing: medium-wide, captures both husband on couch and wife standing, window with hot exterior visible
composition.focal_point: the wife's frustrated yell and the husband's collapsed defeat — the contrast

looks like he's been there for hours and given up
